text: 100g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. everything else is lackluster. not relevant here.
0g of protein. everything else here is pretty flat. also not relevant here.
vitamin k at 10% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

pros first: highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. can help meals feel more complete and usable because fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. small amounts can change a whole meal because that leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

on the weaker side: not the most forgiving cooking fat because great chemistry on paper, but use case is narrower than the headline suggests. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal. calorie density is brutally high because tiny serving errors matter here.
A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
